quiz.question: The test pyramid puts most tests at…
quiz.explanation: Many fast unit tests, fewer integration, fewest E2E.

quiz.question: Mocks are useful when…
options: You want to hit the real payment gateway every time
Isolating a unit from slow/external dependencies
quiz.explanation: Mockito stubs collaborators so you test one unit’s behavior.

quiz.question: @WebMvcTest focuses on…
quiz.explanation: Slice test for controllers; mock services underneath.

quiz.question: Flaky tests usually hurt teams because…
options: They erode trust in the suite and slow delivery
quiz.explanation: Fix or quarantine flakes; never ignore red CI.

quiz.question: AAA in tests stands for…
quiz.explanation: Structure every test: set up, exercise, verify.

quiz.question: A fake repository is useful because…
options: It gives fast deterministic storage for unit/domain tests

problems.title: Unit Test Outline
problems.prompt: Write AAA steps for testing OrderService.placeOrder when amount is negative (should throw).
problems.solution: Arrange: mock deps, order with amount < 0. Act: call placeOrder. Assert: throws IllegalArgumentException; verify repo.save never called.

problems.title: What to Mock
problems.prompt: In a service test, should you mock the database or use @DataJpaTest? Explain trade-offs.
problems.solution: Pure unit: mock repository (fast, isolated). Repository correctness: @DataJpaTest with real SQL dialect/H2. Don’t mix concerns in one test.

problems.title: Write the red test
problems.prompt: For discount: orders >= 100 get 10% off. Write a test name + assert sketch before implementation.

problems.title: Choose double
problems.prompt: PaymentService talks to Stripe HTTP. What do you use in unit TDD and why?
problems.solution: Mock/fake PaymentGateway interface — do not call Stripe in unit tests; keep an integration test separate.
